title: Distichorchis nabawanensis

This classification places it within the monocot group, characterized by having one cotyledon in their seeds. The genus Distichorchis is a rare and lesser-known group within the orchid family. intro: Distichorchis nabawanensis is a species of orchid native to Sabah, Malaysia, characterized by its unique, leathery leaves and small, yellowish-green flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with these conditions. Bioactive constituents of Distichorchis nabawanensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into a tea or decoction, or made into a tincture, allowing for the extraction and utilization of its bioactive compounds.
